description: Readable scaffold for a teacher-approved knowledge item. This is not wired into the student router yet.

id.description: Stable unique id inside a subject pack.

type.description: Kind of teacher-approved content, such as formula, history_person, timeline_event, classroom_note, vocabulary, or concept.

standards.description: Links to standards_map entries such as PS.MATTER.DENSITY.

tool.description: Optional prebuilt app tool that may use this content later. Teachers enable/configure tools; they do not build parsers or routers.

toolConfig.description: Teacher-editable configuration for a prebuilt app tool. Keep parser and routing logic in app code.

studentAnswer.description: Approved answer text and optional scaffolds suitable for the configured reading level.

teacherNotes.description: Private notes for teacher review and future editing.

questionTriggers.description: Teacher-provided phrases that can later help match questions after review.

sources.description: Teacher-approved source labels, URLs, textbook pages, or classroom references.

image.description: Placeholder metadata for future teacher-approved images. No image processing is included in this scaffold.

example.title: Density Formula
example.subject: Physical Science

studentAnswer.summary: Density tells how much mass is packed into a certain volume.
studentAnswer.approvedText: Density is mass divided by volume. Use D = m / V when you know mass and volume.

example.teacherNotes: Use classroom units first: g/mL and g/cm^3.
